personality_one_liner: A warm, high-efficiency, restaurant-grade POS and order-taking system blending clean iOS utility with earthy hospitality tones.

spacing.rule: Generous padding around content. Use standard iOS spacing rhythms. Forms should have inputs with comfortable vertical padding.

navigation: mixed (drawer/sidebar for Tablet, bottom_tabs for Phone)

do's: Use glass on Phone's bottom tab bar
Use glass for the Cart Bottom Sheet header
dont's: No glass on Kanban board cards
No glass on form inputs or dense data tables

screens.name: Role Picker
screens.purpose: First launch screen to select device form factor and role.
screens.layout: Two massive cards stacked vertically on phone, or side-by-side on tablet. Card 1: 'This is the Server (Tablet)' with a tablet icon. Card 2: 'I'm a Waiter (Phone)' with a phone icon. Warm surfaceTertiary backgrounds.

screens.name: Waiter - Join
screens.layout: Centered form. Large monospace input for 4-digit Pairing Code. Standard input for Waiter Name. Sticky primary CTA at the bottom.

screens.name: Waiter - Menu
screens.purpose: Primary order-taking interface optimized for speed and one-handed thumb use.
screens.layout: Sticky Header: Table number selector (large target). Below header: Horizontal scroll view for category pills. Main content: List/Grid of menu items. Each item has title, price, and a large '+' icon button. Bottom: Fixed cart preview summary that expands into a full bottom sheet.

screens.name: Waiter - Cart Bottom Sheet
screens.purpose: Review order and submit to kitchen.
screens.layout: Glassmorphism header with drag handle. List of selected items with quantity adjusters (+/-). Sticky 'Submit Order' button at the bottom.

screens.name: Tablet - Dashboard/Kanban
screens.purpose: Kitchen Display System (KDS) showing live orders.
screens.layout: Sidebar nav on left. Top header shows huge Pairing Code. Main area: 4-column horizontal scroll Kanban board (New, Preparing, Ready, Served). Orders are cards with status color indicators (warning/ochre for preparing, success/sage for ready).

screens.name: Tablet - Menu Editor
screens.purpose: Admin view to add/edit menu categories and items.
screens.layout: Split view. Left pane: Categories. Right pane: Item list with quick-toggle switches for 'Available/Out of Stock'.

role_picker_bg.alt: Rustic cafe table with plates of food

kitchen_tablet_hero.alt: Chef using a tablet to manage kitchen orders

instructions_to_main_agent: Strictly follow the color palette. Do NOT use any blues or purples. The primary accent is Terracotta (#C85A40), success is Sage Green (#4A7C59), and warning is Ochre (#D99B48).
Architecture: Create two distinct flow roots based on the first-launch Role Picker. If 'Tablet', load the Sidebar + Kanban layout. If 'Waiter', load the Stack + Bottom Tabs layout.
Tablet Kanban Board: Must support horizontal scrolling for the board itself, and vertical scrolling for the cards inside each column. Use color-coded left borders on the cards based on their status.

Waiter Phone Menu: Optimize for speed. The 'Add' button on menu items should be large (min 44x44) and easily reachable. Use a fixed Cart Summary component at the bottom of the screen (above safe area) that opens the @gorhom/bottom-sheet.
Implement tactile feedback using the haptics mapping. Speed and confirmation are critical in a restaurant environment.
Pairing Code: Display this prominently in the Tablet header. It must be massive and readable from a few feet away. Mock the pairing logic by saving the code and role to AsyncStorage or context.
When a waiter submits an order, mock the real-time push by instantly reflecting it in the Tablet's 'New' column via shared state/context (simulate WebSocket locally for MVP).
